hidden.title: Hidden widgets ({{count}})
hidden.clickToBringBack: Click a tile to bring it back

customizeDialog.title: Customize Dashboard
customizeDialog.description: Choose which widgets to show and their order.

recentEpisodes.heading: Recent Episodes
recentEpisodes.emptyDescription: Create a series and generate your first episode.

activityTimeline.heading: Recent Activity
activityTimeline.emptyDescription: Create a series and generate your first episode.

upcomingPosts.heading: Upcoming Posts
upcomingPosts.emptyPrefix: Nothing scheduled in the next 30 days.

llmCost.heading: LLM Spend (30d)
llmCost.rateLine: @ ${{prompt}}/${{completion}} per 1k (in/out). Tune in Settings → LLM → Pricing.

recentYouTube.heading: Recent YouTube videos
recentYouTube.empty: No channel videos synced yet. Connect a YouTube channel in Settings → YouTube and Drevalis will pull what's already on it.

firstRun.title: Start here
firstRun.body: Drevalis turns a series idea into a queue of ready-to-publish videos. Create your first series to begin — or fork an example to see how it works.

episodes.subtitle: Browse and manage all episodes across your series.

empty.title: No episodes found
empty.withFilters: Try clearing your filters or search.
empty.noFilters: Create your first episode to get started.

toasts.nothingToGenerateDesc: Selected episodes are not in a draft or failed state.

create.title: Create New Episode
create.topicPlaceholder: What should this episode be about?

delete.title: Delete Episode?
delete.body: This will permanently delete <1>{{title}}</1> and all generated media. This action cannot be undone.

bulkDelete.body: This permanently deletes the selected episodes and all generated media. This cannot be undone.

titleConflict.title_one: Similar to existing video on your channel
titleConflict.title_other: Similar to existing videos on your channel

jobs.subtitle: Monitor and control all generation tasks.

empty.allDescription: Generation jobs will appear here once you start an episode.
empty.filteredDescription: Try a different filter — there may be jobs in other states.

logs.subtitle: Recent pipeline execution events.
logs.clearAria: Clear all app event log entries
logs.clearConfirm:
Clear every entry in the App Events log file?

Pipeline / generation history is NOT affected — only the structured warning/error log file is wiped.

appEvents.title: App events
appEvents.ownerRequiredPrefix: App events require owner access (team mode) or
appEvents.emptyDescription: Warning/error events from the structured log will appear here.

pipeline.title: Pipeline events
pipeline.emptyDescription: Events appear here as episodes are generated.

settings.languageHint: Interface language. Detected from your OS on first run; more of the app is translated over time.
settings.subtitle: Configure backend services, voice profiles, and system settings.

privacy.title: Privacy & Crash Reporting
privacy.intro: Anonymous crash reports help us catch and fix bugs in the alpha faster. No content, file paths, or credentials are ever sent — only the exception type, stack trace, and app version. You can opt out at any time.
privacy.sendReportsHint: When enabled, exceptions in the backend, frontend, and desktop shell are sent to the configured error-tracking backend.
privacy.noDsnConfiguredPrefix: No telemetry backend is configured. Set
privacy.noDsnConfiguredSuffix: to point at your Sentry or Glitchtip project to enable crash reporting.

diagnostics.title: Support Diagnostics
diagnostics.intro: Download a ZIP bundle to send to support when reporting an issue. The bundle contains redacted configuration, health status, recent logs, system info, and the current database revision.

bundle.configPrefix: all settings with secrets replaced by
bundle.logs: last 1000 log lines (if a log file is configured)

dangerZone.title: Danger zone
dangerZone.intro: These actions can’t be undone. Always take a backup first (Settings → Backup → Create backup).

wipe.label: Wipe storage
wipe.description: Deletes every generated file (voice clips, scenes, renders, thumbnails, intermediate caches) from the storage directory. Database rows that reference those files are left untouched — use <1>Reset database</1> for a coordinated wipe of both.
wipe.warning: This permanently deletes every generated file under the storage directory. <1>There is no undo</1> — restore from a backup if you change your mind.
consequences: All voice clips, scenes, renders, thumbnails, and caches are deleted
Database rows referencing those files remain (broken thumbnails until re-rendered)
Re-generation cost is incurred for anything you want back

reset.label: Reset database
reset.description: Truncates every user-data table (episodes, series, jobs, scheduled posts, integrations, API keys, voice/LLM/ComfyUI configs). You stay signed in — auth, license, and migration tracking are preserved.
reset.warning: This truncates every user-data table. You stay signed in, but every episode, series, scheduled post, and saved configuration is gone.<1> There is no undo</1> — restore from a backup if you change your mind.
consequences: All episodes, series, generation jobs, and scheduled posts are removed
All saved API keys, voice profiles, LLM configs, ComfyUI servers are deleted
All connected social platforms are forgotten (you’ll need to reconnect)
The page reloads so cached queries don’t resurrect anything
reset.toastSuccessDesc: {{count}} tables cleared. You're still signed in.

delete.label: Delete account
delete.descriptionOwner: Owner accounts can’t be deleted from this device — the install would be unrecoverable. Use Reset database to clear content instead.
delete.description: Permanently deletes this user account from the install and signs you out. Content you authored stays under its original owner.
delete.warning: This permanently deletes <1>{{email}}</1> from the install and signs you out.<2> There is no undo.</2>
consequences: Future sign-ins for this email will fail until the account is re-created
Content authored by this user stays in the install (no cascade)

network.intro: By default the backend only accepts connections from this machine ({{host}}). Turn this on to reach the API from other computers on your network — useful for managing a testing or staging install remotely.
network.toggleHint: Binds the API to <1>0.0.0.0</1> instead of <2>127.0.0.1</2>.
network.warningTitle: Exposes the full API to your network.
network.warningBody: Anyone who can reach this machine on port {{port}} and has the token below can manage content, YouTube connections, and uploads. Only enable this on a network you trust, and keep the token secret. Remote requests must send <1>Authorization: Bearer <token></1>. The app on this machine never needs it.
network.restartTextTauri: The change is saved, but the API is still bound to <1>{{runtimeHost}}</1>. Restart the backend to start listening on <2>{{bindHost}}</2>.
network.restartTextBrowser: The change is saved, but the API is still bound to <1>{{runtimeHost}}</1>. Fully quit and reopen the app to start listening on <2>{{bindHost}}</2>.
network.rotateHint: Rotating invalidates the current token. Restart the backend for the new token to take effect on LAN clients.
network.exposeDialogTitle: Expose the API on your network?
network.exposeWarning: This binds the backend to your whole network. Anyone who can reach this machine on port {{port}} and has the access token can manage your content, YouTube connections, and uploads. Only enable on a network you trust.
exposeConsequences: The API listens on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) after the next restart
Remote callers authenticate with the bearer token shown here
Keep the token secret; rotate it immediately if it leaks

templates.heading: Video Templates
templates.intro: Reusable configuration presets for voice, captions, music, and visual style.

empty.title: No templates yet
empty.description: Create a template to reuse settings across multiple series.

dialog.createDescription: Define a reusable configuration preset for your series.
dialog.visualStylePlaceholder: Describe the visual aesthetic: color palette, lighting, mood, art style...
dialog.musicVolumeHint: Lower values make the music quieter relative to narration
dialog.setDefaultHint: Applied automatically when creating new series.

delete.title: Delete Template
delete.description: Are you sure? This template will be permanently removed. Series that used it will keep their current settings.

backup.heading: Backups
backup.intro: Full-install archives (DB rows + user media). Safe to move between machines that share the same ENCRYPTION_KEY.

restoreErrors.pickExistingFirst: Pick an existing archive from the dropdown first
restoreErrors.typeRestore: Type RESTORE in the confirmation field to proceed
restoreErrors.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one of database or media to restore
restoreErrors.lostStatusDesc: The worker either never picked up the job or the status TTL expired. Try again.
restoreErrors.completeDesc: {{rows}} rows + {{dirs}} storage dirs. Reload the page to pick up the new state.

restoreStages.uploadCompleteEnqueued: Upload complete — restore enqueued. Waiting for worker…

backup.beforeUnload: Restore upload is in progress. Leaving this page aborts the upload — you will have to start over.

archives.heading: Existing archives ({{count}})
archives.empty: No backups yet. Click <1>Backup now</1> to create your first one.

schedule.title: Auto-backup schedule
schedule.intro: When enabled, the worker creates a backup tarball every day at 03:00 UTC and prunes archives beyond the retention count. Toggle here takes effect at the next tick — no restart needed.
schedule.forcedByEnv: Forced on by DREVALIS_BACKUP_AUTO_ENABLED env var. Unset the env to disable.
schedule.savingUpTo_one: Saving up to {{count}} archive; older ones are deleted.
schedule.savingUpTo_other: Saving up to {{count}} archives; older ones are deleted.
schedule.willKeep_one: When you turn this on the worker keeps the last {{count}} archive.
schedule.willKeep_other: When you turn this on the worker keeps the last {{count}} archives.
schedule.statusDisabledHint: Manual backups still work — use the button below.
schedule.offBoxHint: Want backups off-box? Point <1>DREVALIS_BACKUP_DIRECTORY</1> at a synced folder (Dropbox, OneDrive, Syncthing, an SMB/NFS mount) and the nightly archive lands there automatically.
schedule.enabledToast: Auto-backup enabled — next run at 03:00 UTC

restore.heading: Restore from archive
restore.warning: <1>Destructive.</1> Restoring truncates every user table and overwrites storage files with the contents of the archive. Your current content is deleted. This is the right action when migrating to a new machine.
restore.pickExistingLabel: 1a. Pick an archive already on disk
restore.pickExistingHint: Place multi-GB archives directly in <1>BACKUP_DIRECTORY</1> via <2>docker cp</2> or the host bind-mount, then refresh the list. This skips the browser upload entirely — no proxy timeouts, no navigation issues.
restore.dbLabel: <1>Database rows</1> — series, episodes, audiobooks, configs, OAuth tokens
restore.mediaLabel: <1>Media files</1> — generated videos, audiobook audio, voice previews (can be very large)
restore.allowKeyMismatch: Allow different ENCRYPTION_KEY (OAuth tokens + API keys will need to be re-entered)
restore.uploadingNoLeave: <1>Don't navigate away.</1> The upload is browser-bound — leaving this page aborts it and you'll have to start over. For multi-GB archives, cancel and use "Restore from picked archive" instead.
restore.safeToLeave: Safe to navigate away — the restore runs in the background on the worker. Come back to this page to see progress.

repair.heading: Repair media links
repair.intro: Relink <1>media_assets</1> rows to files on disk after a rough restore or manual copy. Non-destructive: only rows whose current path is broken get updated.
repair.topLevelEmpty: No top-level entries. The storage directory is empty inside the container — whatever you have on the host is not reaching this bind mount.
repair.topLevelHint: If what you see here doesn't match what's in <1>%USERPROFILE%\Drevalis\storage\</1> on your host, the running container was started from a different directory than the one you copied files into. In a terminal at <2>%USERPROFILE%\Drevalis\</2>: <3>docker compose down; docker compose up -d</3>.
repair.unresolvedHelp: <1>bytes nearby</1> — the filename exists somewhere under the storage root but the repair couldn't confidently match it (ambiguous duplicates, missing size on DB row). You can often recover by regenerating the specific scene; safe to ignore otherwise.
repair.missingHelp: <1>missing</1> — the file truly isn't on disk. Regenerate the affected episode or delete the orphan row.
repair.sampleDbEmpty: No media_assets rows with a file_path.
repair.sampleDiskEmpty: Index empty — container sees no files under storage_root. The bind mount is pointing at the wrong directory.

dialog.title: Add Voice Profile
dialog.edgeVoiceIdHint: Microsoft Edge neural voice name. Leave empty for default.

clone.title: Clone voice from sample
clone.intro: Record a 30-60 second clean take right here, OR pick an existing audio asset. ElevenLabs IVC uploads on the first voice test; Piper / Kokoro clones require offline fine-tuning.
clone.tip: Tip: speak at conversational volume, no background music, 30s+.
clone.micDeniedDesc: Fall back to picking an existing audio asset.
clone.samplePickRequired: Pick an existing audio asset or record a sample

youtube.heading: YouTube Channels
youtube.removeConfirm:
Remove "{{name}}" completely?

This deletes the channel AND its upload history from this workspace. It does NOT touch the videos on YouTube itself.
youtube.emptyIntro: No YouTube channels connected. First time? Use <1>Setup wizard</1> to get your Google OAuth credentials and authorize a channel in one flow. Already have credentials? Click <2>Connect Channel</2>.

channel.reconnectTitle: Re-authorize this channel with Google (refreshes OAuth token)
channel.disconnectTitle: Wipe OAuth tokens but keep upload history
channel.removeTitle: Permanently remove this channel and its upload history

videos.syncedEmpty: ✓ Synced — this channel has no videos on YouTube yet.
videos.neverSynced: No channel videos synced yet. Click Resync to pull what's on YouTube.
videos.resyncTitle: Re-enumerate this channel's videos from YouTube
videos.syncStarted: Sync started — checking back in a few seconds

sync.syncQueued: Sync queued — refresh in a few seconds
sync.label: Sync
sync.title: Pull the latest video list + stats from YouTube

social.heading: Social Media Accounts
social.intro: Connect your social media accounts to post content across platforms.
social.loadFailed: Failed to load social media accounts

platform.setupWizardTitle: Walks you through getting TikTok OAuth credentials
platform.facebookPageIdHint: Numeric ID of the Page you want uploads to land on. Get it from facebook.com/{your-page}/about.
platform.instagramAccountIdHint: Business/Creator account ID from Meta Graph — required to create Reels containers.
platform.facebookTokenPlaceholder: Page Access Token (not a user token)
platform.facebookTokenHint: Exchange a user token for a long-lived Page Access Token via Graph API's <1>/me/accounts</1>. User tokens will fail on upload.
platform.publicVideoBaseUrlHint: Instagram Reels need a public HTTPS URL that maps to the storage folder Drevalis writes videos into. Without this, upload will fail.

errors.facebookNeedsPageId: Facebook needs the numeric Page ID.
errors.instagramNeedsAccountId: Instagram needs the Business/Creator account ID.
errors.instagramNeedsPublicUrl: Instagram Reels need a public HTTPS URL that maps to your storage folder.

disconnectDialog.title: Disconnect {{platform}}?
disconnectDialog.warning: This disconnects <1>{{account}}</1> from Drevalis. Re-connecting later goes through the same OAuth or token flow as a first-time setup.
consequences: Uploads currently in progress will fail
Scheduled posts targeting {{platform}} will need to be re-targeted
Saved tokens are revoked locally — Drevalis can no longer publish to this channel

llm.heading: LLM Configurations
llm.emptyDescription: Add an endpoint above (LM Studio, Ollama, OpenAI, or Anthropic) to power scripts.

dialog.apiKeyLabelEdit: API Key (leave blank to keep current)

comfyui.heading: ComfyUI Servers
comfyui.emptyDescription: Add a server above to start generating scenes.

dialog.apiKeyLabelEdit: API Key (leave blank to keep current)

tauri.intro: Auto-update via the Drevalis GitHub Releases feed. The desktop app downloads, verifies, and installs in place.
tauri.noChecksYet: Click "Check for updates" to query the release feed.
tauri.confirmInstall: Download and install the update? The app will close, run the installer, and re-launch automatically.
tauri.installedToastDesc: Restart the app to use the new version.
tauri.trustNote: Update integrity is verified against an embedded public key. Releases must be signed with the matching private key (held by the maintainer / CI) for installation to proceed.

legacy.intro: New releases from the Drevalis team. Updates require an active license.
legacy.forceCheckTitle: Force a fresh check against the update server (bypasses the 6h cache)

reasons.license_required: No active license - activate to receive updates.
reasons.license_revoked: License revoked - renew to receive updates.
reasons.license_expired: License expired - renew to receive updates.
reasons.license_server_not_configured: Offline-only install - updates must be installed manually.
reasons.network_error: Could not reach the update server. Retry in a moment.

legacy.confirmApply: Pull the new images and restart the stack? The app will be unavailable for ~60 seconds. A live progress overlay will show you each phase.

toasts.noDataDescFallback: See the card below for details.

changelog.heading: What's new
changelog.intro: Recent releases from the project's GitHub repo.

license.heading: License
license.intro: Your subscription entitlements and seat information.

manage.title: Manage subscription
manage.intro: Upgrade, downgrade, change payment method, view invoices, or cancel — handled by Stripe's billing portal.

lifetime.title: Lifetime (Pro) — thank you.
lifetime.introWithDate: Your license is permanent. There's no billing portal, nothing to renew. Updates are bundled through {{date}}; after that the app keeps working and you can extend updates anytime from your account page.
lifetime.introNoDate: Your license is permanent. There's no billing portal, nothing to renew. Updates are bundled through your included window; after that the app keeps working and you can extend updates anytime from your account page.

upgrade.title: Upgrade to Lifetime (Pro)
upgrade.intro: One-time CHF 899. Keeps the Pro feature set, never renews. 3 years of updates included. Limited to the first 100 seats.

replace.title: Replace license key
replace.intro: Paste a new JWT to switch tier or extend the subscription.

machines.title: Activated machines
machines.introUsage: {{used}} of {{cap}} seats used on your {{tier}} tier.
machines.introFallback: Seats currently held by this license key.
machines.deactivateSelfTitle: Deactivate this machine (the app will lock)
machines.deactivateRemoteTitle: Free the seat held by this remote machine
machines.confirmDeactivateSelf: Deactivate THIS machine's seat? The app will lock until you paste a new license.
machines.confirmDeactivateRemote: Free the seat held by machine {{machine}}…? The other install locks on its next heartbeat.

seatList.warningBody: The license server returned an error. This is typically a stale license-server deployment — your license still works. Use the refresh button above to retry.

deactivate.title: Deactivate this machine
deactivate.intro: Clears the stored license. The app will lock until a new key is pasted. Equivalent to hitting "Deactivate" next to the highlighted row above.
deactivate.confirm: Deactivate license on this machine? The app will lock until you activate again.

apiKeys.heading: API Keys
apiKeys.intro: Manage encrypted API keys for third-party services. Keys are stored encrypted at rest.

stored.title: Stored Keys
stored.empty: No API keys stored in database.
stored.emptyHint: Keys set via environment variables are shown in Integration Status above.

add.title: Add New Key
add.hint: Your key is encrypted before being stored in the database.

delete.title: Delete API Key
delete.body: Are you sure you want to delete the <1>{{key}}</1> API key? This action cannot be undone. If the service relies on this key, it will stop working.

off.intro: Team mode is <1>off</1>. This install has no user accounts, so everyone with network access can use it. That's fine for a single-user local setup, but not for shared or remote installs.
off.stepSignIn: Sign in at <1>/login</1> — your owner account is created automatically on the first attempt.
off.afterSignIn: Once you're signed in, reload this page to manage additional users.

nonOwner.intro: Only owners can manage team members. Ask an owner on your team to invite or adjust users.

team.intro: Invite collaborators and set what they can do. Owners manage billing and team; editors create and publish; viewers read only.

table.empty: No users yet. Invite someone to get started.

invite.title: Invite a user
invite.validation: Provide a valid email and a password of at least 8 characters
invite.passwordHint: Share this out-of-band. The user can change it after logging in.

edit.title: Edit {{email}}
edit.passwordTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters
edit.selfDemoteHint: You can't demote yourself if you're the last owner — the backend will block it.
edit.activeLabel: Active (disabling blocks login without deleting data)

loginHistory.heading: Recent logins
loginHistory.intro: The last 20 sign-in attempts for your account. If you see an unfamiliar IP or location, use "Sign out everywhere" to revoke all active sessions.
loginHistory.signedOutToastDesc: All other sessions have been invalidated. You have been signed out.
loginHistory.dialogBody: This will immediately invalidate all active sessions on every device, including this one. You will be signed out right away.

twoFactor.heading: Two-factor authentication
twoFactor.invalidCode: Invalid code. Check your authenticator app and try again.

idle.intro: Two-factor authentication adds a second layer of protection. After enabling it, you will be prompted for a code from your authenticator app each time you sign in.

confirming.intro: Scan the link below with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.), or manually enter the secret key.
confirming.storeHint: Store these in a password manager. Each code can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator app.
confirming.codeLabel: Enter the 6-digit code from your app to activate

active.intro: Your account is protected by a TOTP authenticator app. You will be prompted for a code on every sign-in.

disabling.intro: To disable two-factor authentication, confirm your account password.

storage.nearlyEmptyLead: Storage is nearly empty. If you copied media files to your host, make sure the destination is
storage.nearlyEmptyTail: — the app only sees files under the bind-mounted directory. Copying elsewhere (e.g. a sibling folder with a different case, or a drive the compose file doesn't map) won't be picked up.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.intro: Theme presets bundle accent color, display font, border radius, and shadow style. Mode (dark/light) and Activity Monitor position are independent choices.
appearance.currentlyLight: Currently light — high contrast for bright studios.
appearance.currentlyDark: Currently dark — easier on the eyes for long sessions.
appearance.themePresetIntro: Pick a mood — each preset changes fonts, colors, radii, shadows, and icon weight together. Click a card to apply instantly.
appearance.dockIntro: Where the background-jobs bar lives. Top/bottom show a compact tray; left/right show a collapsible full-height rail.

dock.bottomHelp: Classic task-bar strip across the bottom.

palette.placeholder: Jump to a page or action…

channels.intro: Connect the platforms you publish to. Connected channels can be scheduled from the Calendar and targeted by a series.
